description:
You are a digital forensic investigator with severe RSI using Talon Voice. Your task is to build a custom Talon module for dictating regular expressions and then write a Python script to extract IPv4 addresses from a server log. 

Develop a Talon module in C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\forensic_regex\ consisting of three files: regex_setup.py, which must define a 'forensic_regex' module and register a 'regex_class' list; regex.talon-list, which declares 'user.regex_class' with spoken-to-written mappings for digit (\d), word char (\w), whitespace (\s), boundary (\b), any char (.), start (^), and end ($); and regex.talon, which defines a global command 'rx <user.regex_class>' to insert the chosen character class. 

Using this setup, create and execute a Python script at C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Forensics\extract_ips.py that parses C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Forensics\auth.log to find all unique IPv4 addresses. The script must output these unique addresses, one per line, to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Forensics\ips.txt.
